Small Screen Detected

This tool is designed for screens at least 1024px wide. Some controls and panels may not display correctly at your current resolution.

What is this tool?

An interactive tool for exploring brand color strategy in athletic apparel. Compare palettes side by side and investigate how color relates to pricing and discount behavior.

What am I looking at?

Each circle represents a “zone” — a group of products sharing a perceptually similar dominant color. Zones are formed by clustering products using CIEDE2000 perceptual distance, guaranteeing that every product in a zone is within a just-noticeable color difference of every other.

Where does the data come from?

Product images are collected from brand websites over a 16-month period. A computer vision model (YOLO) segments each garment from its background, isolating the apparel for color analysis. The dominant color of each segmented garment is then extracted in LAB color space.

Product image
Segmented

How is the data organized?

Zones are arranged horizontally by lightness (dark on the left, light on the right) and vertically by hue similarity. Circle area is proportional to the number of products in the zone.

Analyses▼
Palette
Color each zone by its LAB centroid.
Discount Frequency
How often products in each zone are discounted, relative to the brand baseline.
Discount Depth
How deeply products are marked down when discounted, relative to the brand baseline.

Enable / Disable
